Best Project Management Tools for Remote Teams

Choosing the right project management tool for a remote team depends on three factors: async collaboration features, integration ecosystem, and per-seat pricing at your team size.

Quick Comparison

Monday.com starts at $8/seat/month. Best for visual workflows and automations. Strongest Gantt charts in the category.

Asana premium is $10.99/seat/month. Best for complex projects with dependencies. Superior subtask handling and custom fields.

Notion offers a free tier, paid plans from $8/member/month. Best for teams that combine docs and projects. Most flexible but steepest learning curve.

What Remote Teams Actually Need

The features that matter for distributed teams are not always the ones vendors highlight. Async updates, timezone-aware deadlines, and offline mobile access matter more than real-time collaboration for teams spread across time zones.

Async Communication

Monday.com offers updates on items with email notifications. Asana has inbox and status updates. Notion uses comments and mentions within pages. All three support basic async patterns, but Asana's inbox is the most polished.

Integration Ecosystem

Monday.com connects to 200+ tools including Slack, Zoom, and HubSpot. Asana integrates with 300+ apps. Notion has fewer native integrations (100+) but its API enables custom connections.

Pricing Breakdown for Teams of 10-50

Tool10 users/mo50 users/mo
Monday.com$80$400
Asana$109.90$549.50
Notion$80$400

Our Recommendation

For remote teams of 10-50 people, Monday.com offers the best balance of features and price. Asana is worth the premium if you manage complex projects with many dependencies. Notion is ideal if your team already uses it for documentation.
